what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the extensions/ tree, prescribing how arbitrary .tsx UI components are generated, build-gated, operator-approved, lazy-loaded, and constrained.
observations: The extensions tree is generated by the system and must never be hand-edited.
New components are proposed by the RHM via propose_extension and applied only after operator approval.
A build-time AST gate (syntax-gate.cjs) rejects non-react import/export specifiers, dynamic import(), require(), and external-URL literals.
Admitted components are lazy-loaded inside an error boundary so a failure degrades to one dead panel, not a white screen.
Every apply is git-committed so revert can recover.
Extensions must not hold authoritative state.
The file deliberately avoids enumerating components because the tree changes and prose would rot.
standouts: Arbitrary-code UI extensions are treated as the most powerful, operator-only self-modification tier.
Safety is layered: AST gate + error boundary + git revert + single-user machine, rather than relying on operator approval as a technical guarantee.
conventions: Follows the constitution document type with YAML frontmatter (type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status).
Uses structured sections: Is, Guarantees, Where new things go, To extend, Seam, Never.
Refers to related constitutions with wiki-style links such as [[runtime — constitution]] and [[canvas — constitution]].
Avoids maintaining a curated list of generated files in prose.
notes: This constitution defines the operator-only, generated .tsx extension tier and its layered safeguards: AST gate, lazy error-boundary loading, and git-revert recovery.
questions: What are the exact AST rules and implementation of canvas/app/syntax-gate.cjs?
How is lazy loading and the error boundary wired in canvas constitution?
What is the precise operator approval workflow and where is it recorded?
What content is in the related Company Map, canvas, and runtime constitutions?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it governs the extensions module by prescribing creation, gating, loading, and recovery.
apparent_themes: Tiered self-modification with increasing power and increasing safeguards
Operator governance as policy, not a technical safety floor
intention_signals: Enable unconstrained UI extensions while containing their risk through build-time gating, runtime isolation, version-control reversibility, and explicit operator approval.
novelty: Treats arbitrary .tsx components as a generated, operator-only extension tier with an explicit AST gate, per-panel error-boundary degradation, and git-revert recovery as core guarantees.
contribution: Provides the governance contract and safety model for the extensions module, specifying that components are generated, AST-gated, lazy-loaded within error boundaries, and recoverable via git revert.
summary_for_embedding: Module constitution prescribing how generated, operator-approved .tsx UI extensions are created through an RHM proposal flow, validated by an AST gate, and lazy-loaded inside error boundaries with git-revert recovery.
intention_for_embedding: To safely enable the most powerful arbitrary-code self-modification tier by layering build-time gating, runtime isolation, operator approval, and version-control reversibility.
